issues.message: Form control is missing an accessible label

evidence.reason: Form control has no accessible label.
evidence.name: full_name

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible <label> connected with for/id. Use aria-label only when a visible label is not possible.

rule.title: Form control missing accessible label
rule.why_it_matters: Students using keyboard navigation, assistive technology, or simplified layouts may not know what information the field expects.
rule.manual_review_notes: Check whether the visual design already implies the label but fails programmatically.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot always confirm labels created dynamically by JavaScript.

issues.message: Link is missing an accessible name

evidence.reason: Link has no usable text, aria-label, title, or image alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add descriptive link text that explains the link destination or action.

rule.title: Link missing accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: Screen reader users hear an empty or meaningless link and cannot decide whether to follow it.
rule.manual_review_notes: Links wrapping only images need useful alt text on the image.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot detect link names injected by JavaScript.

issues.message: Button is missing an accessible name

evidence.reason: Button has no visible text, aria-label, title, or image alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add clear button text or an aria-label that describes the button action.

rule.title: Button missing accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A button without a name is announced as just 'button' by screen readers, so students cannot tell what pressing it will do.
rule.manual_review_notes: Icon-only buttons may look meaningful visually but still need a programmatic name.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot see names added later by JavaScript or icon fonts.

issues.message: Image is missing useful alt text

evidence.reason: Image is missing useful alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add alt text that describes the image purpose, or mark decorative images as presentation.

rule.title: Image missing useful alt text
rule.why_it_matters: Students using screen readers miss the image content entirely, which can hide instructions, diagrams, or required information.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the image is informative or decorative; only a human can judge if existing alt text is actually useful.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static checks cannot judge alt text quality, only whether it exists.

issues.message: Focus stop announces no accessible name

evidence.reason: Chromium's accessibility tree computed an empty accessible name for this focus stop, so a screen reader announces nothing useful about it.

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible label, text content, alt text, or aria-label so the browser computes a usable accessible name for this element.

rule.title: Focus stop announces no accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: Chromium's accessibility tree computed an empty accessible name for this focus stop, so a screen reader user hears at most a role and cannot tell what the element is or does.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm with a real screen reader. When this finding is present, the heuristic browser_focused_control_missing_name check is skipped for the same element, since the computed tree is the stronger evidence.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Based on Chromium's computed accessibility tree in one browser run. It is not a screen reader simulation; NVDA, JAWS, and VoiceOver apply their own rules and other browsers can differ.

rule.why_it_matters: Students using keyboard navigation, assistive technology, or simplified layouts may not know what information the field expects.

limitations: This prototype runs static HTML checks plus a basic keyboard interaction audit.
It does not replace a full human accessibility audit.
Browser mode does not simulate a full screen reader, and accessible names are estimated.

focus_trace.text: Sign up for a library card
focus_trace.accessible_name_guess: Sign up for a library card

announce.name: Sign up for a library card

focus_trace.announcement: button, "Sign up for a library card"

limitations: Browser mode approximates keyboard interaction but does not simulate a full screen reader.
Accessible names are estimated and require manual review.
The announce transcript comes from Chromium's computed accessibility tree in one browser run.
Real screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) apply their own rules and can announce things differently.

announce_transcript.announcement: button, "Sign up for a library card"
announce_transcript.name: Sign up for a library card

limitations: Focus overlay shows observed Tab stops in this browser run.
It does not represent every assistive technology experience.
